items.briefing:
Australian businesses are being warned about a rise in "ClickFix" attacks, where cybercriminals trick users into copying and pasting malicious commands into their own computers — often by pretending to offer a software fix or CAPTCHA prompt. Separately, a new information-stealing malware is impersonating trusted brands like Google, Microsoft, and Apple to compromise Mac devices, so staff should be cautious about software downloads from unofficial sources.

Microsoft has also released patches for Windows, but new zero-day vulnerabilities continue to emerge — meaning unpatched systems remain at serious risk. Ensure your devices are updated as soon as patches are available.

featured.title: Australian businesses warned over ClickFix attacks
featured.quote: Australian small businesses face targeted attacks that bypass standard email and antivirus defenses.
